---
title: "FLicc: Length-based multi-gear catch-curve model"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{FLicc workflow}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.width = 6,
  fig.height = 4
)
```

# Introduction

`FLicc` provides an FLR-compatible implementation of a length-interval catch-curve model for multi-gear fisheries.

The model:
- uses length-frequency data,
- estimates gear-specific selectivity,
- estimates fishing mortality,
- reconstructs equilibrium population-at-length,
- derives stock status indicators such as SPR.

---

# Load package and data

```{r,warning=F,message=F}
library(FLicc)
data("alfonsino")
```

---

# Define life-history parameters

```{r}
lhpar <- FLPar(
  linf = 55.7,
  k    = 0.08,
  M    = 0.162,
  L50  = 31.1859,
  a    = 0.004721956,
  b    = 3.146168,
  s    = 0.7
)
```

---

# Construct the stock object

```{r}
lfds <- lfd_alfonsino
stklen <- stocklen(lfds, lhpar)
```

---

# Inspect observed data

```{r}
plot_lfd(lfds, type = "relmax")
```

---

# Fit the model

```{r}
fit <- fiticc(
  lfds,
  stklen,
  sel_fun = c("dsnormal", "logistic"),
  catch_by_gear = c(0.78, 0.22),
  settings = list(
    CVL      = 0.1,
    GL       = 50,
    catch.sd = 0.05
  )
)
```

---

# Compare observed and fitted compositions

```{r}
plot_plen(fit)
plot_plen(fit, by_gear = TRUE)
```

---

# Inspect selectivity

```{r}
matplot(fit$tmb_data$Lmid, fit$report$Sel, type = "l", lwd = 2,ylab = c("Selectivity"),xlab=c("Length (cm)"))
```

---

# Extract key outputs

```{r}
fit$report$spr
fit$report$spr0

```


---

# Summary

This vignette demonstrated how to:
- prepare inputs,
- fit a multi-gear length-based model,
- evaluate diagnostics,
- interpret selectivity and SPR,
- extend across years.

`FLicc` is particularly suited for data-limited and multi-gear fisheries.
